name: Achievement Badges
description: Unlockable viewing achievement badges for Jellyfin users.
overview: Unlockable viewing achievement badges for Jellyfin users.

versions.changelog: v1.9.2 - Two fixes for v1.9.x: (1) Admin Classic toggle was broken because body[data-ab-style=revamp] persisted from prior /achievements visits and leaked Revamp CSS via ancestor selectors, hiding the badge grid. Admin's applyPref now syncs body[data-ab-style] alongside #AchievementBadgesPage. (2) Friends drawer last-watched was inaccurate for users with thousands of plays. Bumped the LastWatched query Limit from 50 to 500 so we actually find the most recently played item. With the existing 90s cache, the higher Limit costs nothing in practice.

v1.9.1 - Friends drawer surface tier lightened in Revamp (was too dark). Bumped L* of the drawer canvas, friend rows, and FAB button by ~5pp each (16/22/26 instead of 11/15/18) so each tier reads distinctly against the next. README updated with full v1.9.0 features section.

v1.9.0 - UI Revamp + Security A+ + plugin-wide efficiency overhaul. Cumulative release covering all work since v1.8.10. New features: full Revamp UI for the standalone /achievements page (magazine hero, conic donut, asymmetric stats grid, chapter tabs, control-panel filter strip, ambient orb + film grain), Friends drawer Revamp following the same Classic/Revamp toggle, Offline - last watched X line on offline friends, HideLastWatched privacy preference, HMAC-SHA256 webhook signing for receiver-side authenticity verification. Backend efficiency: debounced Save in AchievementBadgeService and MessagingService, FriendsService LastWatched cache with 90s TTL and play-event invalidation, AuditLogService in-place trim and tail-walk read, WriteIndented disabled on production stores, embedded resource caching, Cache-Control public max-age 86400 immutable on assets with version-only cache busting (browsers now actually cache between page loads), middleware marker fast-path. Security A+: default class-level rate limit, AdminAuditLogFilter capturing every elevation action, CSP and security headers on the anonymous profile-card endpoint, 34 xUnit security regression tests, GitHub Actions CI runs tests + dotnet vulnerability scan + gitleaks on every push, expanded SECURITY.md with full threat model. Three medium audit findings fixed: input clamps on GetActivityFeed/GetMessageThread/GetConvMessages, WebhookUrlValidator fails closed on DNS errors.

Group chat moderation: PROMOTE/DEMOTE ADMINS + KICK MEMBERS. Adds an Owner role (the original creator, gold pill) plus zero-or-more Admins (green pill, stored as adminIds[] on the Conversation model). In the View members modal, admins now see a kebab on each non-self/non-owner row with applicable actions: 'Make admin' (any admin), 'Remove admin' (owner only), 'Remove from group' (any admin can remove non-admins; only the owner can remove other admins). New backend endpoints POST /admins/{id} and DELETE /admins/{id} mirror the auth: owner-or-admin can promote, owner-only can demote, kick rules match. Backward compatible: existing groups deserialize with empty adminIds[] so the owner is the only admin until they explicitly promote someone.

New feature: VIEW MEMBERS in group chats. The chat menu (kebab) now has a 'View members' entry for groups, and the '… members' text in the group-chat header is clickable. Both open a new modal listing every participant in the group with avatar, name, a 'You' pill on yourself and an 'Admin' pill on the group's creator. Members are sorted you-first, then admin, then alphabetical. Pulls a fresh participant list from GET /conversations/{id} so newly added members show up immediately, and falls back to the friends list to resolve names for non-friend members.

Follow-up to v1.8.7: v1.8.7 patched the DELAYED startup EvaluateAllProfiles pass, but MISSED the synchronous Load() path inside the AchievementBadgeService constructor, which also calls EvaluateBadges with default args (silent=false, stamp=utcNow) for every profile during plugin load. That's the true origin of the update-toast cascade — every badge re-evaluated on load was stamped with 'now' and fired a webhook + audit + toast. Fix: Load() now passes silent=true + DateTimeOffset.UnixEpoch so any unlocks that happen during profile load never count as toast-worthy (same 1970 cutoff trick used by EvaluateAllProfiles). Also addresses the 'data reset' perception reported by the community: no counter data is actually being wiped on update (atomic writes + 3-layer recovery chain remain untouched, SyncDefinitions only adds, EvaluateBadges only increments). The perceived 'reset' was the cascade of old unlocks re-toasting, which made it look like achievements were being re-earned. With v1.8.7 + v1.8.8 together, the cascade should be fully eliminated.

Two community-requested fixes. (1) UPDATE CASCADE BUG FIXED: installing a new plugin version was replaying every unlocked achievement as a toast, sometimes dozens in a row. Root cause was the startup EvaluateAllProfiles pass stamping newly-unlocked badges with 'now minus 5 minutes' — if the client's last-seen timestamp was older than 5 minutes (normal for any user who wasn't actively polling), every one of those startup unlocks passed the since-cutoff and fired a toast. Fix: startup backfill now stamps with DateTimeOffset.UnixEpoch (1970), which is unconditionally older than any client's last-seen, so those unlocks never replay as toasts. Users still get the unlocked state on the badges page; toasts are reserved for unlocks that happen while playing. Also switched the per-browser SHOWN_IDS tracking from sessionStorage to localStorage (survives tab close + plugin update; capped at 400 entries with oldest-first eviction). (2) MUTE DURING PLAYBACK: two new user prefs added to the standalone settings panel — MuteToastsDuringPlayback (hides achievement popups while a video is playing) and MuteToastSoundDuringPlayback (suppresses the unlock sound during playback). Both default to TRUE so the sensible behavior kicks in automatically; users who love the popups can toggle them off. Detection uses body.playingVideo class + visible playing <video> element. The existing minimum-toast-rarity filter is unchanged and still discoverable in the Toast & Sound section.

Bug fix: the floating Friends button was appearing on Jellyfin's login / server-picker / wizard pages. Added pre-auth URL checks (/login.html, #!/login, #!/addserver, #!/selectserver, #!/wizard, /quickconnect, body.classList.contains('loginPage')) plus an ApiClient-ready guard so the button only shows once there's an authenticated user. Also drops the drawer automatically if it was somehow open when the page becomes pre-auth.

Small UX pass: New Group button now uses the same tab-button visual language (transparent with purple-blue hover gradient, matching border/radius) so it reads as part of the Friends/Messages tab row, sitting full-width right below the tabs instead of looking like an outlier chip. README updated with a proper Messaging section covering 1:1 + groups, attachments, read receipts, edit/delete, block, notifications, prefs, and storage details.

Three UX fixes. (1) WHATSAPP-STYLE MESSAGE MENU: click anywhere on your own message bubble to open a compact Edit / Delete dropdown. Menu is positioned inside the chat pane so it can't clip out on narrow screens. The 3-dot button still appears on hover as a visual hint but the entire bubble is the click target. (2) BIGGER BUBBLES: max-width raised 78% -> 88% so long messages don't wrap after 3 words on narrow drawers. (3) UPLOAD FIX (second attempt): strip Content-Type from ALL auth headers (previous pass only stripped from the default JSON header), and surface the actual HTTP status + server message on failure so upload errors are debuggable. (4) NEW GROUP BUTTON: centered, wider (80% up to 300px), soft outlined pill with hover lift — replaces the right-aligned chip.

Polish pass from 1.8.2 feedback. (1) RENAME GROUP now uses our own styled modal instead of the browser native prompt() dialog, which looked ugly on some themes. (2) IMAGE UPLOAD FIXED — the upload endpoint now reads Request.Form directly (was failing silently because our default authHeaders() set Content-Type: application/json, which broke multipart boundary detection). Frontend now strips Content-Type from the upload request so the browser auto-sets the correct multipart/form-data boundary. (3) READ RECEIPTS are much more legible — bigger, bolder, with a high-contrast green (#38e07b) + subtle glow for read, and nearly-full opacity white for delivered. No more blending into the bubble background. (4) NEW GROUP BUTTON restyled from a big purple gradient pill to a small subtle outlined chip in the tab header, right-aligned. (5) EDIT + DELETE actions: replaced the pill-tray with a single small 3-dot button (right of own bubbles on hover) that opens a proper dropdown menu with Edit / Delete. Matches Telegram / Discord conventions. Clicking outside the menu closes it.

Big release: group chats, image attachments, read receipts split into delivered/read, flicker-free chat, top-center notifications, and message-action polish. GROUP CHATS: conversation-based model replaces pair-keyed messages (1.8.1 data auto-migrates on first boot). 'New group' button on the Messages tab lets you create a named chat with 2+ friends. Headers show member count; gear menu gets rename + leave options. IMAGE ATTACHMENTS: paperclip button next to the chat input accepts PNG / JPG / GIF / WebP up to 8MB, server verifies the magic bytes so you can't smuggle non-images, attachments render inline with click-to-zoom lightbox, and get deleted from disk when their message is deleted/cleared. READ RECEIPTS FIX: single gray tick for delivered, double blue tick for read — matches the convention from every other messenger. Per-participant readBy map replaces the single readAt scalar so group receipts work too. NO MORE FLICKER: chat view diffs messages by a content hash and only repaints when something actually changed, preserving scroll position. Same trick for the Messages tab thread list. MESSAGES TAB AUTO-UPDATES: 8s poll while the tab is active, plus the existing 20s drawer-wide poll refreshes the list too — no more 'last message only updates when you open the chat'. MESSAGE ACTIONS RE-POSITIONED: edit / delete buttons now float above the bubble in a pill-shaped tray instead of drifting off to the side. Delete icon is a proper trash icon instead of an X. TOAST TOP-CENTER like a normal OS notification, not bottom-right. Per-peer mute still localStorage-only; group chats substitute Rename / Leave for Mute / Block in the gear menu.

Messaging v2: Messages tab, edit/delete, block, notifications, sounds, gear menu. (1) NEW MESSAGES TAB next to Friends/Requests/Find, shows conversation threads newest-first with avatar, last message preview, timestamp, and unread count. Click a row to open the chat. Tab has its own blue unread-count pill. (2) BETTER UNREAD STYLING: the per-friend indicator is now a small pulsing red dot on the chat button (not a big red text blob next to the name). Requests badge also moved to the top-right corner of the Requests tab instead of inline. (3) GEAR MENU in chat header with Clear conversation, Mute/Unmute notifications for that user (localStorage, no server round-trip), and Block user. (4) MESSAGE EDIT/DELETE: hover your own messages to reveal pencil + X. Edit window is 24 hours, max length same as sending (1000). Edited messages show an '(edited)' marker. (5) READ RECEIPTS: a double-check appears next to the last of your messages the peer has read. Already tracked server-side via readAt. (6) BLOCK USER: bi-directional hard stop on messages. Stored per-user in UserAchievementProfile.Preferences.BlockedUsers. Either side blocking blocks both directions; existing messages are kept but new ones are rejected. (7) NOTIFICATIONS: subtle Web Audio chime on inbound message, in-app slide-in toast (bottom-right), browser notifications via Notification API when tab isn't visible and permission granted. Per-conversation mute toggle in the gear menu. (8) USER PREFERENCES (in the standalone page settings panel): MessageNotifications, MessageNotificationSound, MuteMessageNotificationsDuringPlayback — the mute-during-playback flag suppresses notifications while Jellyfin's video player is active. (9) CONVERSATION CAP RAISED from 500 to 2000 messages per conversation (still FIFO). (10) ADMIN CONTROL: existing FriendsEnabled toggle still gates the whole feature. Defer group chats + attachments to v1.9 — those need their own data model and file-storage layer.

Xbox-style 1:1 messaging between friends. Every friend row in the drawer gets a new chat-bubble icon; clicking it slides in a message pane over the friends list with the peer's avatar, online status, conversation history, and a text input at the bottom. Messages are stored server-side in plugins/configurations/achievementbadges/messages.json with atomic writes, auto-read-on-open, unread-count tracking for per-friend red dots, and a combined unread indicator on the floating friends button. Rate limited to 20 msgs/min per sender with a 1000-char max and a 500-message FIFO cap per conversation. Friendship is enforced: non-friends can't message each other (simple-mode treats every server user as a friend, consistent with the rest of the feature). 5 new endpoints under /Plugins/AchievementBadges/users/{userId}/messages/* covering unread-count, threads list, per-thread fetch, and send. Drawer open starts a 6s in-chat poll and a 20s drawer-wide unread poll so updates land without refresh.

Desktop calendar layout restored + mobile leaderboard fit. DESKTOP (> 640px): heatmap + streak calendar return to the old full-width aspect-ratio-scaled look with no day/month labels and no horizontal-scroll chrome — exactly how it was before v1.7.15. The renderer still emits the inline grid-template-columns=repeat(N,1fr) needed for cells to stretch across the container. MOBILE (<= 640px): keeps v1.7.15's fixed 12px cells, horizontally-scrollable wrapper, Mon/Wed/Fri day labels, localised month labels, and GitHub-style color legend, so cells stay readable at phone widths. Switch is CSS-only via a @media (max-width: 640px) block that uses !important to override the desktop inline grid-template-columns. LEADERBOARD mobile fit: podium + rows were clipping out of the tab container on phones because each podium column maxed at 170px × 3 + gaps = ~520px. Added a @media rule that shrinks the podium (medal 1.4em, name 0.8em, val 0.72em), removes the 170px column cap, compacts row padding, shrinks the rank gutter, and truncates overflowing user names with ellipsis. Panel cards now have overflow:hidden + min-width:0 on flex children so nothing else can bleed past the card edge. Panel padding tightened 1.1em → 0.75em on phones.

Mobile calendar polish + form-based quest editor. CALENDAR POLISH: heatmap + streak calendar redesigned for readability. Cells now have a fixed minimum size (14px desktop, 12px mobile) with the grid wrapped in a horizontally-scrollable container so they never shrink to unreadable widths on phones. Mon/Wed/Fri day-of-week labels on the left, month labels above (localised), Monday-aligned first column, leading blank cells so weeks line up with actual calendar weeks. GitHub-style color legend (Less → More + 'max N/day') replaces the old single-line hint. Hover scale bumped from 1.3 → 1.35 for easier targeting. QUEST EDITOR: replaced the raw JSON textarea with a proper form. Each built-in quest now has a disable checkbox that strikes it through and greys it out when ticked — no more hand-typing 'daily-late-night, weekly-weekend' into a comma-separated list. Each custom quest is an inline card with labelled inputs: Id, Title, Icon (Material icon name), Description, Metric (15-option dropdown), Target number, Reward number, + Delete button. '+ Add daily quest' and '+ Add weekly quest' buttons at the bottom. Old raw-JSON editor kept as an Advanced expandable section for power users / bulk paste — any edit to it overrides the form on save. +32 i18n keys for the calendar labels + quest form (721 total per file). Version stamp bumped to 1.7.15.

Real fixes for the three things still broken in 1.7.13. SIMPLE MODE: Requests + Find tabs now hidden via a CSS class (#abFriendsDrawer.ab-fd-drawer-simple [data-ab-hide-in-simple]{display:none!important}) — switched from inline-style toggling because `!important` beats any stray display override from other scripts / themes. Class is re-asserted on every drawer open and on simple-mode flip. Same for the Requests/Find panes. FRIENDS FEATURE DISABLED: admin flipping FriendsEnabled=false now physically removes the button + drawer + backdrop from the DOM via _destroyAllFriendsDom() instead of just setting display:none (which was racing with syncFriendsBtnVisibility and applyCorner). No button visibility at all when off. FORCE-HIDE SHOWCASE on refresh: root cause was public-config requiring auth. On page refresh, fetch sometimes fired before ApiClient mounted → 401 → null response → fallback path set _showcaseEnabled=true → showcase stayed visible. Two fixes: (1) public-config is now [AllowAnonymous] (it's just feature flags + default language + the already-public custom logo), (2) resolveShowcaseEnabled keeps the flag null instead of assuming enabled when BOTH fetches fail, so the 5s periodic retry catches up. Watchdog also installed unconditionally on script load, so as soon as the flag resolves to false the DOM gets swept immediately. Reconcile interval tightened to 5s. Version stamp bumped to 1.7.14 so stale-script cleanup works on upgrade.

DUPLICATE FRIENDS BUTTON + everything-broken fix: v1.7.12's cache-busting kicked in mid-flight, so users with the old index.html in browser cache ended up loading BOTH the old sidebar.js (no ?v=) AND the new sidebar.js (?v=1.7.12) — and both IIFEs tried to mount their own floating button + drawer. The old IIFE had no idempotency check, so you got two buttons (one at bottom-left from the old default, one at the new corner), the drawer was being fought over by two scripts, the simple-mode tabs were only hidden by the newer script on its own drawer (while the old one still showed them), and the blank-drawer / force-hide-broken symptoms were all side effects of the two IIFEs colliding. Fix: every DOM node we create now carries data-ab-ver='1.7.13'. On mount we first remove any abFriends* element that doesn't have the matching version marker, so a newer script always wins and older cached scripts get their artifacts cleaned up on the next mount pass. Plus a belt-and-braces ensureFriendsDrawer() race-fix (reserves the mount slot synchronously before the async config fetch so double-calls can't both pass the guard). After installing v1.7.13 please do ONE hard refresh (Ctrl+Shift+R on web, force-kill + reopen the app) to flush the stale cached index.html; from v1.7.13 onwards the ?v= cache-buster will handle upgrades automatically.

Force-hide + friends visibility + cache-busting. FORCE-HIDE: now installs a <style> tag with `display:none !important; visibility:hidden !important;` on #ab-sidebar-showcase + #ab-header-badges whenever the showcase is disabled — so even if some stale script re-creates the DOM between the 250ms watchdog sweeps, the user never sees it. Removed automatically when the showcase is re-enabled. FRIENDS BUTTON visibility conflict fixed: v1.7.11 reconcile was setting btn.style.display='' every 15s which undid the dashboard/playback auto-hide. Now the admin-enabled check is tracked separately (_friendsAdminEnabled), syncFriendsBtnVisibility checks BOTH gates (admin enabled AND not-on-dashboard AND not-in-playback), and the reconcile loop just calls the sync function instead of setting display directly. shouldHideFriendsBtn also checks location.pathname now (Jellyfin config pages live at /web/ConfigurationPage not just hashes). FRIENDS SIMPLE MODE: Requests + Find tabs are always rendered but carry a data-ab-hide-in-simple attribute, toggled via display:none when simple mode flips. Previously they were conditionally OMITTED at mount time, so toggling simple mode at runtime left the old buttons around until a refresh. Active tab also falls back to Friends if the user had Requests/Find selected when simple mode turned on. CACHE-BUSTING: /client-script/{name} now sends Cache-Control: no-cache, must-revalidate. WebInjectionService adds ?v=<plugin-version> to every injected script URL so browser caches effectively reset on plugin upgrade — root cause of 'you STILL haven't fixed X' after every release was browsers serving stale JS.

Force-hide propagates faster + avatar glitch fixed + admin friends controls + corner picker. FORCE-HIDE PROPAGATION: periodic re-resolve dropped from 60s to 15s + runs on hashchange + visibilitychange so admin ForceHide takes effect across every open session without a refresh. AVATAR GLITCH on online friend: the `.ab-fd-avatar.online` CSS rule used `background:` shorthand with a green gradient which clobbered the inline `background-image:url(...)` of the Jellyfin profile picture. Split into `background-color` + `background-image` separately, and dropped the online gradient entirely — online state now shows purely via the green inset ring + status dot. Profile images render cleanly with or without the online indicator. ADMIN FRIENDS CONTROLS (new): Feature Controls section now has two new toggles — FriendsEnabled (master kill switch, floating button + drawer don't mount at all when off, endpoints return empty) and FriendsSimpleMode (drawer hides Requests + Find tabs and the Friends tab shows every user on the server instead of a follow-list — useful on small family servers where the request/accept flow is friction). USER FRIENDS BUTTON CORNER: new preference (bottom-left, bottom-right, top-left, top-right) — drawer slide direction follows the button anchor. Broadcasts a 'ab:friends-corner-changed' event so the button moves instantly without a refresh. +9 new i18n keys x 8 languages (689 total).

Showcase toggle race-fix + admin force-hide propagation + SVG specific error + SVG <use> allowed + README. SHOWCASE TOGGLE clunky hide — when you flipped Show equipped showcase OFF it sometimes kept showing until a hard refresh. Root cause: the 'ab:showcase-pref-changed' broadcast fired BEFORE the pref POST had landed on disk, and sidebar.js responded by re-fetching /preferences which returned the OLD (true) state. Now sidebar.js reads detail.show directly from the event payload and applies it without a server round-trip, so the DOM is removed instantly. ADMIN FORCE-HIDE not taking effect on other users' open sessions until refresh — the admin doesn't broadcast to other tabs. Added a 60s periodic re-resolve in sidebar.js that re-fetches public-config + user prefs, so admin-side changes propagate within a minute without requiring any of the affected users to reload. SVG upload — split SanitizeAndEncodeSvg into a tuple-returning variant so the specific sanitizer error (disallowed element, bad URI, > 100 KB, not well-formed XML) now shows up in the SvgWarning returned to the admin instead of a generic 'rejected' message. Relaxed SvgSanitizer to allow <use> elements as long as their href/xlink:href is a same-document anchor (#id) — blanket-blocking <use> was rejecting legitimate icon SVGs. External URIs in <use> still blocked. README updated with the privacy toggles (appear offline, hide now playing), Jellyfin avatar support, relaxed online check, and compact request rows.

Admin translations definitive fix + friend avatars + appear-offline/hide-now-playing + compact request rows + relaxed online check. ADMIN PAGE i18n: stopped relying on ApiClient for URL resolution — now parses window.location.pathname to find the server root (works on reverse-proxy subpaths like /jellyfin/ without ApiClient available), fires an UNCONDITIONAL first-paint load from localStorage/navigator immediately, then upgrades via ApiClient + user-prefs once mounted. The translate pass also now re-invokes loadCatalog once the dict lands so the badge grid re-fetches with the correct ?lang= and shows localised titles without needing a manual reload. FRIEND AVATARS: each row now shows the user's Jellyfin profile image (fetched via apiClient.getUserImageUrl) as a background-image; initials fall back when no image is set. ONLINE CHECK relaxed: v1.7.5-1.7.8 only showed friends as online if they had an active playback controller, so casual browsing didn't count. Now also counts sessions whose LastActivityDate is within 15 minutes. APPEAR OFFLINE: new user setting — friends always see you as offline even while you're browsing. HIDE WHAT I'M WATCHING: second new setting — friends see you as online but not the series/episode you're watching. Both toggles live in the Privacy section of the user preferences panel. REQUEST ROWS: Accept + Decline buttons collapsed to icon-only with tooltips so the incoming-request row no longer visually bloats. LOCALE PARITY: +4 new settings.* keys x 8 languages (680 total keys per file).

EQUIPPED BADGES LOCALIZE: GetEquippedBadges was bypassing BadgeLocalizer — it returned raw profile.Badges clones with English Title/Description regardless of the user's saved Language pref. So switching languages would flip every other UI string but leave the equipped row in English. Now Localize runs on each clone using profile.Preferences.Language. Same fix applied to BuildEquippedPreview so the leaderboard / compare / public equipped dots also translate. ADMIN PAGE i18n made robust: the translator IIFE now waits for Jellyfin's ApiClient to mount (up to 5s, polled every 80ms) and passes the auth token + fully-qualified URL to the /translations fetch — previously the optimistic early fetch could 404 under reverse-proxy subpath setups or fail CORS/auth checks, leaving the page stuck in English even when localStorage had 'pt'/'fr'/etc. Also seeds from localStorage immediately while waiting for the server round-trip, so the first paint is already in the right language.

REAL fix for hero 'Loading...' stuck: applyStaticTranslations was called AFTER loadAll had populated abSaSub (and other text-only data-i18n placeholders) — and because the element had no child elements, my v1.7.5 'skip populated containers' guard didn't cover it. So every translation pass re-set textContent back to the literal 'Loading...' string. Now the translator explicitly skips data-i18n='common.loading' keys so they're never clobbered post-render. Subtitle / stats / rank-bar labels now actually stay populated. ADMIN PAGE category + rarity labels now localised via client-side lookup of the existing rarity.* and category.* i18n keys — the enable/disable grid's section headings and per-badge rarity chips stop being English for French/etc. admins (badge.Title/Description were already passed through ?lang= server-side since v1.7.6; now the client-side labels match). README updated with the Friends drawer, rarity-% chip, data-loss recovery chain, and credit to @frenchyx24 for the hand-translated French.

Hero 'Loading...' stuck fix + admin badge translations + rarity % chip. FIXED hero subtitle stuck on 'Loading...': loadAll's Promise.all had 6 non-guarded fetches (users/{id}, summary, equipped, leaderboard, server-stats, rank). Any single rate-limit or 5xx would reject the whole Promise.all and the .then callback — which is the ONLY thing that populates the hero subtitle, stats boxes, and a dozen other panels — never ran. Now every fetch has its own .catch returning a safe default so the UI always finishes rendering. ADMIN BADGE TRANSLATIONS: /admin/badge-catalog now accepts ?lang= and runs every definition through BadgeLocalizer.Lookup so the enable/disable-badges grid shows French titles for French admins, etc. Admin page passes the localStorage 'achievementBadgesLang' key automatically. NEW rarity percentage chip: every badge card now shows the % of users on your server who've unlocked that badge (groups icon + number). Colour-coded green >=50%, amber 10-50%, red <10%. Fed by a new /badges/rarity-stats endpoint with a 5-minute server-side cache so it doesn't burn cycles on every page load.

Data-loss recovery + friends visibility + admin-page translation fixes. DATA RECOVERY (big): Load() now walks a chain — primary badges.json → badges.json.bak → badges.json.recovery — and only quarantines the primary if ALL three fail. Save() while quarantined writes to .recovery instead of skipping, so in-session play progress persists even when the primary is busted (previous behaviour felt like a reset on every restart since no new data was being written anywhere). .bak is rotated on every successful Save. Admin can now see load status via the /test endpoint (LastLoadSummary is exposed). FRIENDS button now hides on /dashboard and /plugins pages (admin screens have their own crowded UI) and during media playback (detected via playing <video> element or body.playingVideo class) — reappears automatically when you leave. ADMIN PAGE translation: waits up to 2.5s for Jellyfin's ApiClient to mount before reading the user's language preference (was failing silently when apiClient was still initialising on first admin-page load, falling back to browser language = usually English). Also fixed the same textContent-nukes-children bug the standalone page had — admin containers like the badge catalog, custom badge editor, audit log no longer get wiped when translations apply.

The one where I actually found the BADGE TRANSLATION root cause. The badges.{fr,es,de,it,pt,zh,ja}.json files I've been shipping since v1.6.0 were NEVER ending up in the main DLL — MSBuild's resource pipeline saw the *.<culture>.json naming pattern and shunted them into culture-SATELLITE assemblies (fr/Jellyfin.Plugin.AchievementBadges.resources.dll etc.). BadgeLocalizer calls GetManifestResourceStream on the main assembly → null → silent fallback to English for every non-English user, no matter how much French I added to the JSON files. Fixed by adding WithCulture=false + explicit LogicalName on each badges.*.json in the csproj. The main DLL now actually contains 'Premier contact', 'Seigneur du Binge-watching', etc. FIXED 'Loading settings...' stuck: applyStaticTranslations called textContent= on every [data-i18n] node, which NUKES child elements. The settings container had data-i18n='settings.loading' on the outer div, so every language-change pass wiped the rendered settings panel and left the bare 'Loading settings...' placeholder. Now applyStaticTranslations skips any node that already has child elements, so populated panels stay populated. Same guard protects abSaLb, abSaActivity, abSaWrapped, abSaPrefs, abSaRecords, abSaPrestigeLb, abSaServerStats, abSaBank, abSaDailyQuest, abSaWeeklyQuest. FIXED equipped-showcase STILL showing: disk patch fixes from v1.7.3 handled fresh installs, but Jellyfin's aggressive index.html caching meant upgraders kept being served the old bloated-inline-script version for hours. sidebar.js now installs a PERMANENT watchdog (setInterval every 250ms + MutationObserver) that removes any #ab-sidebar-showcase or #ab-header-badges element as soon as it appears when the showcase toggle is off. Idempotent, belt-and-braces, cheap. FRIENDS button moved to SIDEBAR.JS so it's visible on EVERY Jellyfin page, not just the achievements tab. Position: bottom-left corner, z-index high enough to float above Jellyfin's own controls. Full code relocated; standalone.js now has zero friends code.

Follow-up fixes after 1.7.2 feedback. FIXED (finally) equipped-showcase pills persisting after disable: the real bug was in WebInjectionService — on upgrade it saw the old '<!-- achievementbadges-bootstrap -->' marker from pre-v1.7.3 and skipped re-patching, leaving the bloated inline script from v1.6.x still running alongside the new gated sidebar.js. Now: markers are versioned with <!-- ab-vX.Y.Z -->, the injection block has start AND end markers, and on every upgrade we strip ALL previous bootstrap blocks (including legacy marker-only ones — scans from the start marker to the first </script> after `client-script/enhance`) before re-injecting. FIXED language dropdown reverting to Default: the handler stopped awaiting savePromise before rendering so a server hiccup could roll the <select> back. Now re-renders from the LOCAL chosen value immediately, hard-pins the <select>.value after render as belt-and-braces, and refreshes badges in the background. Also mirrors the pick to localStorage so it survives page reloads. FRIENDS drawer redone: moved floating button from bottom-right to TOP-LEFT (vertically centered on desktop, top-left corner on mobile — was in the way of Jellyfin's own controls); full visual overhaul — rounded-rect button, gradient avatars with online-dot overlay, proper request/accept buttons with icons, blurred backdrop, left-side slide animation, empty-state illustrations. Slides in from the LEFT now to match the button position. ADMIN page now follows the user's Achievement Badges language preference: resolves via (user preference → admin default → localStorage → navigator → English), writes the pick to localStorage when changed on the standalone page so next admin load is snappy. README credits beefed up — @frenchyx24 credited by name with a link to his issue and a thank-you for the full 171-badge French translation.

CRITICAL data-loss fix + settings-stuck fix + friends drawer + French translations from frenchyx24. FIXED (CRITICAL): Load() in AchievementBadgeService was wrapping the entire deserialise+migrate+evaluate loop in a single try/catch that wiped the in-memory dict on ANY exception. The next Save() then overwrote badges.json with {} — permanently destroying every user's badges, counters, prestige, bought, equipped, pinned, and compare history. Now: parse failure quarantines the file to badges.json.corrupt-<ts> and refuses to Save() for the rest of the process; per-profile errors during SyncDefinitions/EvaluateBadges no longer poison the whole load; Save() writes badges.json.bak before each atomic rename. If you were hit by this in 1.7.0/1.7.1, restore from your Jellyfin backup or the quarantined .corrupt-* file. Sorry. FIXED: 'Loading settings...' stuck forever after changing language — the language change handler awaits the pref POST and fetches fresh prefs before re-rendering, with a safety catch so the panel never sticks. FRIENDS: rebuilt as a floating bottom-right button + Xbox-style right-side drawer (instead of a tab). Three sub-tabs: Friends / Requests / Find. Bi-directional friendship now: POST /friends/{id} sends a request, POST /friends/{id}/accept creates the mutual link, DELETE removes/declines from both sides. Red badge on the floating button when you have pending incoming requests. Type-to-search user picker in the Find tab. TRANSLATIONS: merged all 171 hand-translated French badge titles + descriptions from frenchyx24 (issue #5) — validated and sanitised on import (id regex, 500-char cap, no HTML/CR/LF). +17 new friends-drawer keys × 8 languages. Thanks @frenchyx24!

Follow-up release with friends + translation fixes + polish. NEW: Friends feature — one-sided follow model, see online/offline status pulled from Jellyfin's ISessionManager, each friend's equipped badges, and what they're watching right now (series + episode). Mutual indicator when both sides follow each other. New Friends tab on the achievements page. TRANSLATIONS: fixed language dropdown showing 'English' after changing to French — the <select> re-render was passing the stale prefs object so the old language was still highlighted. Fixed badge titles staying English after language change — race between preference-save and badge re-fetch meant BadgeLocalizer saw the old language; language-change now awaits the pref POST before re-fetching. Added localised badge categories (Binge → Marathon, Getting Started → Premiers pas, etc.) and rarities (Common → Commun, Legendary → Légendaire, etc.) to all 8 language files — covers the Stats tab category rings, badge grid meta line, activity feed, category filter dropdown. 32 category keys + 6 rarity keys × 8 languages = 304 new translation entries (646 total UI keys per language). POLISH: README updated with v1.7.x features + credits section thanking xdnewlun1, Uenify, and the French community for fork contributions. Native-speaker translation PRs welcome.

Large community-requested update. FIXED: disable-equipped-badges toggle now actually hides sidebar pills AND header dots (middleware was injecting a second unconditional copy that bypassed the flag; middleware now defers to sidebar.js which is properly gated). NEW: see other users' equipped badges everywhere — new public preview endpoint /profiles/{userId}/equipped, rendered as coloured dots next to every leaderboard row, podium spot, category leaderboard entry, and compare-view user name (respects all privacy toggles). NEW: admin quest customization — add or edit daily / weekly quests from the Jellyfin admin page, with GET/POST /admin/quests endpoints, 100-entry caps, field clamping, and a 'disabled quest Ids' list to remove built-in quests your server can't satisfy. TRANSLATIONS: genuinely comprehensive now — all 171 built-in badges translated to 7 languages (fr, es, de, it, pt, zh-CN, ja); UI translation files ballooned from 553 → 614 keys each covering the achievement profile, stats snapshot, personal records, heatmap, streak calendar, prestige leaderboard, notification prefs, server stats, compare, leaderboards, recap, quests, admin page; added a local tr() helper on the admin page and translated ~50 status/error strings + data-i18n attributes for placeholders/labels/titles. SECURITY AUDIT: HIGH — compare endpoint now requires caller to be one of {userIdA, userIdB} or an admin (UserOwnershipFilter ignored {userIdA}/{userIdB} tokens, letting any authenticated user spam another user's on-disk CompareHistory); stored XSS fixed in admin leaderboard / audit-log viewer / all badge title + rarity + description + category spots across index.html, standalone.js, showcase.html (admin-authored custom badges + crafted Jellyfin usernames could inject script). MEDIUM — equipped preview now respects HideFromLeaderboard and HideFromCompare (not just ShowEquippedShowcase); public /profiles/{id}/equipped endpoint tightened (was requiring BOTH HideFromLeaderboard && HideFromCompare to opt out); profile-card endpoint returns unified 'unavailable' HTML for missing users (was leaking existence via 200 vs 404); GiftScore validates recipient via IUserManager, clamps amount to [1,10000], rate-limits, and emits an audit log; EquipBadge, UnequipBadge, PinBadge, EquipTitle, LoginPing, BuyBadge now rate-limited with user-60-per-min. LOW — Webhook HttpClient set to AllowAutoRedirect=false to stop SSRF-via-redirect to cloud-metadata / localhost; /test endpoint no longer leaks plugin version + web-injection patched paths to anonymous callers unless EnableDebugEndpoints is explicitly enabled (default now off); middleware exception path restores response body more safely. POLISH: showcase preference toggle now broadcasts a custom event so sidebar pills + header dots update without a hard refresh. Thanks to the French user who sent the bug list + feature requests via fork comments.

Critical auth + logo fixes + showcase toggle + security audit. FIXED: 401 error on achievements page refresh / back from shareable card — auth header fetch now waits up to 2s for ApiClient to populate, retries once on 401 transient races. FIXED: Xbox logo upload silently failing — base64 detection now checks for < character before attempting decode, warnings surfaced in UI. NEW: Disable equipped badges showcase — per-user toggle in settings + admin server-wide override. Hides the sidebar showcase strip, header badge dots, and equipped slots section. Achievements sidebar link stays visible. SECURITY: 4 additional issues fixed from internal audit — exception leak in profile card endpoint, unbounded limit/weeks query params, SaveUserPreferences allowlist validation, PinBadge id validation + 50-entry cap. NO critical issues found beyond the ones already fixed in v1.6.0.

Major release. SECURITY: full hardening pass — IDOR fix via UserOwnershipFilter, profile card 404 + GUID validation, custom badge sanitizer, webhook URL validator (SSRF), client-side XSS escaped in shell.js + sidebar.js, completionPercent clamping, middleware buffer cap, rate limiting on user/anonymous endpoints + cooldowns on prestige/recompute, audit log username redaction option, SECURITY.md documenting CSRF mitigation. ADMIN: custom Xbox toast logo upload with XML-parsing SVG sanitizer (XXE-safe, blocks script/iframe/use/event handlers). I18N: 8 languages (en/fr/es/de/it/pt/zh/ja) with ~220 UI keys + admin page translated + first ~50 most-common badges localized (rest fall back to English, community contributions welcome). Translation hot-swaps on language change. Thanks to xdnewlun1 (Techno Cricket, CCDC) for responsible disclosure.

Security hardening release. Enforce per-user authorization on user-scoped API endpoints. Admin-only endpoints unchanged. Validate and sanitize admin-supplied custom badge definitions, webhook URLs, and imported profiles. Escape user-controlled strings in the achievements UI renderers. Return 404 for unknown users on the public profile card endpoint and stop creating empty profiles on anonymous reads. Additional input bounds on playback completion API. Cap response buffering in the injection middleware. Plus: admin can now upload a custom SVG to replace the Xbox logo in toasts. Expanded translations to 8 languages (English, Français, Español, Deutsch, Italiano, Português, 中文, 日本語) with ~220 keys covering most UI. Thanks to xdnewlun1 (Techno Cricket, CCDC) for responsible disclosure.

Multi-language support (English, French, Spanish), profile card back button, CASSETTE/VINYL bug definitively fixed. Root cause of the icon text bug: sidebar.js bypassed the icon allowlist, so equipped badges with invalid icon names rendered raw text in the sidebar showcase pills and header badge dots. Now applies the same Material Icons allowlist as standalone.js. Also fixed 5 badge definitions with invalid icon names (vinyl→album, cassette→music_note, sports→audiotrack, mosque→public, rocket→rocket_launch). Profile card: added a top-left back button with iOS safe-area support (44x44 touch target). i18n: ~50 UI strings translated to en/fr/es, user can pick language in settings, admin sets server default. Badge titles/descriptions not yet translated (future work).

Self-only quests/activity in privacy mode + Extreme Spoiler Mode. Force Privacy Mode no longer hides the Quests and Activity tabs — users can still see their own quests and their own activity. Activity feed in privacy mode auto-filters to the current user only, hides the user dropdown, renames heading to 'Your activity'. New Extreme Spoiler Mode (user pref + admin force): completely hides locked badges from the list instead of just blanking descriptions. Spoiler and Extreme Spoiler are separate toggles in both user settings and admin Feature Controls. If both on, extreme wins.

Recently unlocked filter + admin-forced settings hide user toggles. New 'Recently unlocked' option in the badge filter dropdown (sorts by UnlockedAt desc, top 10). Force Privacy Mode now ALSO hides the user's privacy toggles + activity feed checkbox (since admin enforces them server-side). New admin setting ForceSpoilerMode — when enabled, spoiler mode applies to all users and their toggle is hidden. Pattern: when admin forces something, the user toggle hides with 'Enforced by admin' note. Individual kill switches also hide corresponding user toggles (e.g., LeaderboardEnabled=false hides HideFromLeaderboard).

Ultra privacy mode. When ForcePrivacyMode is enabled: Leaderboard, Compare, and Activity tabs are completely HIDDEN (not just emptied), server stats section removed from Stats tab, user dropdowns removed, admin page leaderboard and stats panels hidden. No sign that other users exist anywhere in the UI. Individual kill switches also work independently — disable just Leaderboard and only that tab disappears. If user is on a hidden tab when flags change, auto-switches to My Badges.
Welcome banner redesigned as minimal frosted glass with gold left accent bar — no emoji, just clean text. Hidden users can now see their own activity feed entries while remaining hidden from others.

Welcome banner redesigned (gradient bg, sparkle icon, gold accent, works in both themes). Rarity borders boosted for common/uncommon/rare/epic (2px solid + box-shadow). Activity feed privacy now filters ALL entries (old + new) unconditionally. Plugin catalogue image set to the blue achievement badge.

Definitive bug fix release. Activity feed privacy: fixed JSON key casing mismatch — both save functions now delete stale keys before setting canonical values, added [JsonPropertyName] attributes to all preference properties. Spoiler mode: GetBadge() single-badge endpoint now applies spoiler logic (was only applied in the list endpoint). Badge rarity borders: fixed class name mismatch (CSS used rarity-*-border but JS applies ab-r-*-border). Rank progress bar: added ID to track element for light-theme override. Welcome message: new public-config endpoint (no admin auth) + banner display on achievements page. CASSETTE/VINYL text: added 350+ Material Icons allowlist, invalid icons fall back to emoji_events. Light theme: bumped all secondary text to rgba(0,0,0,0.6)+ minimum, fixed showcase cards, equipped empty state, profile link, hero icons, inline-style progress tracks.

Comprehensive fix for all remaining bugs. Admin Feature Controls section added to the EXISTING admin page (Pages/index.html) instead of a separate config page — all 10 controls now visible with load/save via feature-config API. Activity feed privacy fixed: saveSettingsPrefs now sends BOTH camelCase and PascalCase keys since C# deserializer wasn't matching camelCase fields. Theme persistence fixed: theme now re-applies every time the page renders via loadAll(), not just on first visit. Light theme: massive CSS override pass covering heatmap cells, SVG text in rings/radar/clock, quest card backgrounds, badge rarity borders, streak header numbers, stats section values, prestige button, and all remaining invisible elements.

Three bug fixes. (1) Admin Feature Controls now visible in Dashboard: configPage.html was wrapped in a full HTML document structure which Jellyfin can't inject — stripped to bare page fragment. (2) Activity feed privacy now works: both save functions (settings panel + notification prefs) were each POSTing only their own fields, causing the other panel's fields to reset to defaults. Both now GET current prefs first, merge changes, then POST the complete object. Also sends both camelCase and PascalCase field names for activity feed toggle. (3) Light theme comprehensive fix: added overrides for streak heatmap cells, personal records, server stats, notification preferences, badge card borders, compare stats labels, category ring labels, activity feed rows, goal cards, settings sections, muted text, and input fields.

Fix light theme visibility, activity feed privacy, and add admin config page. Light theme: ~50 CSS overrides added for rank progress bar, streak pills, leaderboard podium, recap stats, wrapped cards, badge cards, compare bars, quest text, toggle labels, and all other elements that were invisible against white backgrounds. Activity feed privacy: fixed field name inversion — the toggle now correctly maps 'Hide from activity feed' (checked) to appearInActivityFeed=false. Admin config page: new 'Feature Controls' section in Dashboard plugin settings with all 10 admin controls — kill switches for leaderboard/compare/activity/prestige/quests, force privacy mode, max equipped badges, restrict badge visibility, disable badge categories (comma-separated), and custom welcome message.

Frontend settings panel + themes. Gear icon on the achievements page tab bar opens a full settings panel with 3 sections: Toast & Sound (5 controls), Privacy (4 toggles), Display & Features (5 controls including theme dropdown and equipped badge slots). All settings auto-save on change — no submit button, instant feedback. Three achievement page themes: Default (current look), Dark (deeper blacks, reduced glow), Light (white cards, dark text, fully adapted tabs/buttons/inputs). Theme applies immediately on selection and persists via user preferences. Enhance.js now respects MinimumToastRarity preference — if set to 'rare+', common/uncommon unlocks are silently skipped (still marked as seen so they won't re-appear). Sound toggle was already wired.

Full preferences + admin controls backend. 14 user preferences: toast on/off, sound on/off, confetti, milestones, minimum toast rarity filter (all/rare+/epic+/legendary+), achievement page theme (default/dark/light), spoiler mode (hides locked badge descriptions), equipped badge slots (1-10), auto-equip new unlocks, push notifications, and 4 privacy toggles (hide from leaderboard, compare, activity feed, prestige board). 10 admin controls: kill switches for leaderboard/compare/activity/prestige/quests, force privacy mode, max equipped badges, restrict badge visibility, disable badge categories (e.g. Late Night for family servers), and custom welcome message. Privacy filters injected into GetLeaderboard, CompareUsers, GetActivityFeed, GetPrestigeLeaderboard. Spoiler mode blanks locked badge descriptions. Auto-equip adds newly unlocked badges to equipped slots automatically. DisabledBadgeCategories filters badges from evaluation and display. New admin endpoints: DELETE /admin/users/{userId}/reset, GET/POST /admin/feature-config. Frontend settings UI coming in next release.

Toast queue now shows one at a time (was 3 stacked). Multiple unlocks queue up and play sequentially so each gets its full animation + sound. Also added a DOM-based fallback for playback detection — if window.Events isn't available (some Jellyfin builds), a MutationObserver watches for .videoPlayerContainer appearing and triggers a poll burst. This should fix toasts not firing during video playback on setups where the Events API isn't exposed.

Fix green background on diamond spritesheet. The original CodePen spritesheet PNG had a solid green (#42AF0D) background baked into every pixel with no alpha channel. The diamond sparkle content was drawn on top of this green. When rendered on non-green rarity colors (e.g. legendary gold), the green background showed through. Fixed by processing the PNG to make all green-dominant pixels (G>100, R<120, B<80) fully transparent, preserving only the diamond sparkle content (18% of pixels). The rarity color now shows through correctly behind the diamond.

Diamond spritesheet for legendary/mythic toasts + diagnostic endpoint. Legendary and mythic unlocks now show the CodePen diamond spritesheet animation (147 frames, served from a new binary asset endpoint) instead of the trophy. The trophy is hidden for these tiers and replaced with the rotating/sparkling diamond. The client-script endpoint now also serves PNG/MP3/SVG embedded resources (not just JS). The /test endpoint now returns full injection diagnostics (WebPath, IndexFound, IndexPatched, PatchedPath, LastError, embedded resource availability) so users with enhance.js loading issues can troubleshoot.

Complete toast rewrite — carbon copy of the CodePen animation with per-rarity colors. The entire toast CSS and HTML structure has been replaced to exactly match the standalone HTML demos we built from Uenify's Xbox One Achievement CodePen. Structure: .circle (75x75 sphere gradient, pulse ring ::before/::after) containing Xbox logo + trophy SVG images (both inlined as base64), .banner-outer > .banner (355px pill with horizontal+vertical gradient, shimmer sweep, text slide). Xbox logo visible at idle, fades out at 23% as trophy fades in and rotates (rotateY). All keyframes are the exact CodePen originals (circle_grow_move, scale_circle_1, banner_animate, textSlide, rotate, shimmerSweep). Both achievement sounds inlined: regular Xbox 360 chime for common/uncommon, rare Xbox One chime for rare/epic/legendary/mythic. Per-rarity colors applied via CSS custom properties on circle + banner backgrounds.

Full CodePen-style toast port. The Xbox logo now appears inside the circle at idle and swaps to the trophy icon when the banner expands — matching the original Uenify CodePen animation we spent hours perfecting in the standalone HTML demos. Xbox fades out at 22% of the cycle, trophy fades in at 23%, trophy fades back out at 92%, Xbox returns at 93%. Trophy rotates via rotateY while visible. The Xbox SVG path is inlined directly in the toast HTML (no external dependency). Trophy starts at opacity:0 and is absolutely centered inside the circle so both icons occupy the same space and the swap is seamless.

Achievement unlock sound + richer toast visuals. The Xbox 360 achievement chime now plays when a badge unlocks (inlined as 7KB base64, respects user prefs EnableSound). Banner now has a horizontal gradient (darker edges → transparent center) layered with the existing vertical sheen, giving it noticeably more depth instead of a flat single-color fill. All six rarity base colors darkened slightly so the 'bright' shift during banner expansion (24-85% of the animation) is more visible — the color change from base to bright now reads as a real transition instead of being barely noticeable.

Fix skewed ETA calculations. ETAs were computed by dividing remaining progress by (counter / DaysWatched), where DaysWatched is the number of distinct days you actually watched something. This made ETAs wildly optimistic because it assumed you watch every day. A user who's been on Jellyfin for 365 days but only watched on 67 of them would get an ETA based on their watching-day rate (3.1/day) instead of their calendar-day rate (0.57/day), making a 4-year achievement look like it's 256 days away. Now uses calendar days since first recorded watch date (earliest entry in WatchDates) as the denominator, so ETAs reflect actual real-world pace including off-days.

Hide the header equipped-badge row on narrow phone screens. 5 equipped badges at 30px each ate ~170px of horizontal space, which on phones pushed the hamburger menu and profile icon off the right edge of the Jellyfin header bar and made them unreachable. The sidebar entry still works, so users can still get to their achievements page through the nav drawer — the header strip is now desktop / fold / tablet only. Hidden via a CSS media query (max-width: 640px) #ab-header-badges { display: none !important; } injected into <head> alongside the sidebar script. Applied to both the middleware inline fallback and the new disk-injected sidebar.js so the fix covers every client path.

Fix sidebar entry missing on the Jellyfin Android app. Previous versions relied entirely on SidebarInjectionMiddleware to rewrite index.html at runtime, but the Android app pre-fetches / caches the HTML file in a way that bypasses the ASP.NET Core middleware pipeline — so the script tags never reached it. New WebInjectionService hosted service patches index.html directly on disk at plugin startup (same approach StarTrack uses), baking three script tags before </body>: sidebar, standalone, and enhance. The on-disk patch is atomic (write-to-tmp then rename) and idempotent (marker detection). SidebarInjectionMiddleware stays registered as a fallback for setups where the web directory isn't writable, and now skips rewriting if the marker is already present to avoid double-injection. Also refreshed Pages/sidebar.js with the modern anchor-strategy that lands next to Plugin Settings — previously sidebar.js carried the legacy broken selector chain.

Hygiene pass from a full-plugin audit. Every JSON store (user badges, playback completion, audit log) now writes atomically: serialize to '<file>.tmp' then 'File.Move(..., overwrite: true)'. Previously 'File.WriteAllText' was used directly, so a process kill or power loss mid-write would truncate or corrupt the JSON file and wipe every user's unlocked badges / playback counters. File.Move is atomic on NTFS when source and target are on the same volume, which they are because the temp file is a sibling. Also fixed a StreamReader in SidebarInjectionMiddleware that was never disposed, with an explicit leaveOpen:true so the encapsulating MemoryStream 'using' still handles cleanup.

Sidebar entry is now anchored next to Jellyfin's own 'Plugin Settings' / 'Plugin Pages' item instead of wherever the first '.navMenuOption' happens to be — which, depending on which other plugins are installed, could land us inside a 3rd-party section like Kevin Tweaks. The injector walks every '.navMenuOption', text-matches 'Plugin Settings' / 'Plugin Pages', and inserts ours directly after it. Fallbacks: Home item, then first nav item (previous behaviour) as last resort.

Root-cause fix for the missing sidebar. The previous versions were injecting correctly — the problem was the script never reached the browser in the first place. SidebarInjectionMiddleware's path check only matched requests whose URL contained the literal string 'index.html', but Jellyfin web is typically served at '/web/' with no filename, so every shell-page load bypassed the middleware and no script ever got injected. The middleware now intercepts any plausible HTML GET (anything that isn't an obvious static asset, /api/, /Plugins/, /emby/, etc.) and gates on Content-Type 'text/html' before rewriting. Also fixed two adjacent bugs: (1) compressed responses (gzip/br) are now passed through untouched instead of mangled by a UTF-8 text read, and (2) Content-Length is cleared instead of being pre-set to avoid a Kestrel write race on some configurations.

Sidebar entry actually shows up now. v1.5.19's anchor strategy assumed every Jellyfin layout wraps nav items in '.navMenuSection' divs, but modern 10.11.x web flat-renders '<a class=navMenuOption>' children directly inside '.navMenuOptions' with no section wrappers, so the previous query returned nothing. The injector now finds any existing '.navMenuOption' (Home, Movies, etc.) and inserts ours as a sibling in the same parent — cloning the layout we found instead of guessing the wrapper structure. Also clones the existing item's class list so we inherit Jellyfin's exact styling. Insert position: right before the first sibling matching Sign Out / Settings / Dashboard / Manage Server, falling back to append. Retry loop extended from 15s to 60s for slow / cold-cache loads, and the MutationObserver is now throttled to 250ms so nav rebuilds don't thrash.

Two bug fixes. (1) Sidebar entry restored: the 'Achievements' link was failing to inject on themes/setups that don't expose '.mainDrawer-scrollContainer .navMenuOptions'. The injector now anchors on the parent of any '.navMenuSection' (theme-agnostic, matches every Jellyfin layout) and inserts itself as a proper navMenuSection just above the User/Sign Out section, with broader fallback selectors. (2) Toasts during playback: removed the CSS rule that hid '#ab-toast-container' whenever a video player was on screen, so unlocks earned mid-watch now actually appear, and the container is force-promoted to the top z-index layer. Polling cadence dropped from 30s to 8s, and the enhance script now binds Jellyfin's playbackstart/playbackstop/playbackprogress events to fire a poll burst within ~1s of an unlock so toasts surface near-instantly while watching.

Toast polish. Glow halos dialed way down across all tiers (was neon/blinding, now a subtle colored ring around the drop shadow). Radial sphere highlight on the circle softened. Banner sheen softened. Confetti now originates from the toast's actual position (bottom-center) instead of the top-right corner - the anchor uses the toast container's getBoundingClientRect. Banner.svg polished: one clean gold color instead of cycling through 6 rarities, slower trophy rotation (6s instead of 3s so it feels elegant not frantic), smoother spline easing on banner expand/collapse, single softer pulse ring instead of two, inner cup shadow on the trophy for depth, tighter text letter-spacing.

Big Xbox toast refinement. Circle now has a radial highlight/shadow gradient so it looks like a sphere instead of a flat disc. Banner has a vertical top-highlight to bottom-shadow sheen and an inset rim-light. Each unlock plays a diagonal shimmer sweep across the expanded banner (legendary + mythic get a second sweep). Per-rarity glow intensifies up the ladder: epic gets a stronger halo, legendary and mythic get huge multi-layer colored glows plus a drop-shadow on the trophy icon. Rarity is now applied via a class (ab-xb-{rarity}) so CSS can target each tier directly. Banner.svg stripped further - pure transparent background, no decoration, just the looping achievement animation itself (also replaces the literal '\u2013' escape bug that was rendering as text).

Xbox toast rebuilt closer to the original Adam Cosman codepen. Circle is now a solid base color with two separate pulse rings (lighter ::before + darker ::after) instead of a smooth gradient, and the circle/banner color-shifts to a brighter shade during the 24-85% expanded keyframes (mirrors the codepen's #39960C -> #42ae0e transition). Each rarity gets 4 coordinated shades (base/lighter/darker/bright). Banner has a subtle top-highlight / bottom-shadow sheen so it reads with depth without looking neon. GitHub README banner stripped of all decoration (stars, corners, rarity bar, header text) and is now just the looping Xbox achievement animation itself, cycling through all 6 rarity tiers.

Xbox-style unlock toast now uses 3-stop vertical gradients (lighter -> base -> darker) per rarity instead of flat fills, so Mythic/Legendary/Epic read with proper depth and no longer look neon and flat. Rarity color palette also realigned to the canonical rainbow used elsewhere in the plugin (#9fb3c8 / #34d399 / #60a5fa / #a78bfa / #fbbf24 / #f43f5e). GitHub README banner replaced with a looping animated SVG that literally reproduces the Xbox achievement popup: circle expands into a banner, trophy rotates inside, rarity cycles through all six tiers on a 30s master loop so visitors see every gradient.

Admin panel now has a 'Test unlock toast' section with 6 buttons (Common through Mythic) that preview the Xbox-style animation at the bottom-center without needing to play content or inject counters. Uses a new exposed window.abAchievementTestToast() helper from enhance.js. Also: new animated SVG banner for the GitHub README with a rotating gradient diamond (8s rotation, 3s pulse), pulsing radial glow, twinkling star field, and a shimmer sweep across the title.

Xbox One style unlock toast: expanding circle with rotating trophy grows into a full banner showing 'Achievement unlocked' / 'Rare achievement unlocked' + Gamerscore + badge title, then collapses back to a circle and disappears. 10.5s total animation, positioned at bottom-center. Rarity tier colors the banner. Rare/Epic/Legendary/Mythic tiers also get a glow effect + confetti timed to the banner reveal. Fix: showcase pill + header dot icons now render in white so they actually contrast with the rarity-colored border (previously icon and border were the same color). Header dots also have a subtle rarity-tinted background now.

Sidebar + header showcases now use real Material Icons matching the rest of the UI. Previously the inline injection script in SidebarInjectionMiddleware still had the old emoji iconMap fallback, so equipped badges showed up as approximated emoji (moon, alarm, etc) on the home page even though the achievements page itself had switched to material icons in v1.5.7. Also improved the visual: header dots are now 30px with a rarity-colored glow, sidebar pills use proper icon + text spacing with rarity color on both elements.

Chase modal now randomizes suggested items (different list every click) and filters properly by badge metric - movie badges get movies, series badges get series, episode badges get episodes, genre badges use Jellyfin's genre filter. 35 new badges across Prestige (15/25/50), Score Economy (500k/1M lifetime), Morning Ritual, Combo Master, Library Completionist, Badge Hoarder, Director Devotee, 7 Decade specialists (60s-20s), 7 Day-of-Week specialists (Mon-Sun), Binge Marathon (5/10/15h in one day), Library Specialist (100/500 items in one library), Actor devotee extremes, Ultra Movie Marathon, Binge Everest. New metrics: LifetimeScore, BestComboCount, LibrariesAt100Percent, BadgesUnlockedPercent, DecadeItemsWatched, DayOfWeekItemsWatched, MaxLibraryItemCount, MaxMinutesInSingleDay. New counters tracked on every playback: decade dict, day-of-week dict, minutes-by-date dict.

Pinned badges replace Smart Goals as the 'Working on' row (show only what you pick, no unrealistic auto-suggestions). Rarity-colored borders on every badge card - the whole grid now color-codes visually. Leaderboard Score sub-tab auto-loads when you open the Leaderboard tab (no more clicking between tabs to trigger it). 25 new badge definitions: 11 genre specialists (Horror, Comedy, Drama, Action, Sci-Fi, Animation, Documentary, Crime, Romance, Thriller, Fantasy), 3 streak extremes (200/365/500 days), 2 late-night extremes (500/1000 sessions), 2 rewatch extremes (100/500), 2 total-time extremes (2000/5000 hours), Year Rounder (365 days), and 4 Prestige tier badges (reach prestige 1/3/5/10) using a new PrestigeLevel metric.

Heatmap + streak calendar reworked as CSS grid with aspect-ratio:1 so cells stay perfectly square at every range. Wrapped tab redesigned with a huge gradient hero banner showing the year total, then 3 labelled sections (Your numbers / Your highlights / Your favorites) with accent-bordered cards, emoji icons, hover-lift and list cards for top genres/directors/actors. Smart Goals row on My Badges tab showing 3-5 daily suggestions tied to your closest badges, click to open the chase modal. Co-watch detection: when two users watch the same item within an hour, both get +20 score bank bonus. Compare history: last 10 comparisons saved per user with quick-access pills. Per-user notification preferences (toasts, milestone toasts, confetti, activity feed visibility, co-watch bonus opt-in) controlled from the Stats tab and honored by enhance.js.

Pin button moved to footer (no longer overlaps points chip), restyled with subtle blue accent when active and a 'PINNED' label corner badge on pinned cards. Streak calendar now has a fire-themed header showing current streak, best streak, and active days with an animated flame and gradient typography. Hero banner shows a current-streak chip when you have a streak going. Profile card adds a current streak stat. Achievement ETA: backend computes per-badge days-remaining-to-unlock from your viewing velocity (current/days_active * remaining), displayed as an orange chip on locked badge cards. Yearly Wrapped tab: Spotify-style stat cards for any past year showing items / movies / episodes / active days / best streak / hours plus top genres / director / actor / biggest day / top month / top day-of-week, with a year selector.

Big polish + skipped-features pass. Compare row layout fixed: values on outer edges, bars meeting at center label, winner highlighted green. Activity feed now paginated (20 per page) with user filter dropdown (All / specific user). Recent unlocks panel removed from Stats tab (duplicated by Activity). Streak calendar - full year SVG grid showing every day you watched. Material Icons font replaces emoji iconMap for ALL badge icons - real movie/tv/dark_mode/etc icons rendered cleanly. Achievement milestones: 25/50/75/100% catalog completion fires a special gold milestone toast with triple confetti burst. Title system: pin any unlocked badge as your Title shown under your name in the hero. Pinned badges: pin button on every card, pinned float to top of grid, pinned cards have gold accent. Category filter dropdown on My Badges (auto-populated from your loaded badges). Toast queue (max 3 visible at once, rest queued). Reduced motion mode: respects prefers-reduced-motion AND has a manual toggle on the achievements page that disables confetti.

Big feature drop: side-by-side profile compare with VS bars + winner highlights + summary pills, server-wide activity feed of recent unlocks across every user, click any locked badge to open a modal showing actual library items that would help you finish it (uses chase backend), per-category progress rings on Stats tab, 24-hour watch clock radial chart, personal records grid (16 stats), prestige leaderboard tab on Stats, recent unlocks panel, watch heatmap fills the panel width properly. Backend additions: /compare/{a}/{b}, /activity-feed, /users/{id}/records, /users/{id}/category-progress, /leaderboard-prestige, /users/{id}/recent-unlocks-v2, /users/{id}/watch-clock, /users/{id}/pin/{badgeId}, /users/{id}/title.

UI overhaul pass. Home screen widget removed completely (was flashing then being clobbered by other plugins rebuilding the home DOM). Profile card fully redesigned: conic-gradient spinning avatar ring, tier-colored glow, large gradient score number, progress bar with tier accent, hover-lift stats grid. Leaderboard now has a 3-person podium at the top with gold/silver/bronze bars + medals, and rows 4-10 as sleek list with progress bars. Server stats now a 6-card grid with emoji icons and hover-lift. Recap top genres / directors / actors rendered as bar charts instead of boring bullet lists, with a hero card showing total items + mini-grid for movies/episodes/days/unlocks. Badge points chip larger and more prominent (+88% font + glow). Heatmap SVG now uses preserveAspectRatio=none so it stretches to fill the container width at larger ranges. Prestige button polished: gold-to-pink gradient, no harsh border, bigger padding, nicer hover lift, cleaner disabled state. Confetti burst on achievement unlock toasts (28 particles in rarity color + rainbow). Toast itself slightly larger with better typography and rarity glow.

Silent scan so Watch History backfill no longer spams unlock toasts - scanned badges use their original play date as UnlockedAt so the toast poller ignores them. Profile card now server-side rendered with all data baked into the HTML - no more Failed to load. Item detail ribbon disabled by default (opt-in via admin UI). Multiple concurrent quests - 3 daily + 3 weekly at the same time, rotated deterministically per day/week. Each badge card now shows its point value (+X pts) scaled by your prestige level (+50% per prestige). Fancy animated prestige button with gradient, shine effect and glow. Heatmap range dropdown 30/90/180/365 days. Updated README with full feature list, all API endpoints, and prerequisites table.

Feedback fixes. Profile card link now uses absolute path (was 404). Toast notifications seed LAST_SEEN to now on first load and dedupe shown unlocks via sessionStorage so they no longer replay on every refresh. Header badge pills now hidden while the video player is active. Watch heatmap is wired to real data from a new /watch-calendar endpoint and colors cells by intensity. Director/actor tracking now uses ILibraryManager.GetPeople instead of reflection - top directors/actors will populate in Recap after a rescan. Sort badges by rarity / progress / title. Filter by individual rarity tier. Dedicated Quests tab with both daily AND weekly quests (12 daily templates, 8 weekly templates). Admin page restyled with modern expanding sections, dark form controls, focus rings. Plugin now auto-evaluates every user's badges on startup so new badges unlock automatically after updates without needing Scan All Users.

Diagnostic + resilience hotfix for sidebar/header injection missing on some servers. Added verbose console logging from inline injection script so we can see exactly which step fails. Added aggressive retry loop (every 1s for 15s plus 500ms/2s delayed injections) so other plugins rebuilding the nav menu can no longer permanently clobber our entry. Expanded nav container selectors to match more Jellyfin themes. Added server-side logging to the middleware so we can confirm if it's even firing for /web/index.html requests.

Security fix + massive feature set. SECURITY: every admin endpoint now requires RequiresElevation policy so regular users can no longer change plugin config. Prestige system (reset rank after Legend to earn a prestige star, lifetime score preserved). Score bank earned per playback with combo multipliers (10%+ per consecutive watch). Spend score bank to buy any locked badge. Gift score between users. Daily quest rotation with 8 templates, shared deterministically across users, completing rewards the bank. Recommendation engine chases the badge - shows exactly which items to watch to complete a specific badge. Export / import profile JSON. Per-badge reset. Admin progress injection for testing. Audit log of all unlocks. Visual badge editor form (no more JSON). Challenge templates (1-click create monthly, Halloween, New Year, Summer). Stats tab with genre radar, heatmap placeholder, score histogram. Rank themes unlock CSS overrides at Enthusiast, Binger, Connoisseur, Maestro, Legend, Immortal. Slack webhook format auto-detected from URL.

Massive expansion. Rank system with 10 tiers (Rookie to Immortal) based on achievement score, shown with progress bar on user profile and home widget. Unlock toast notifications poll every 30s during playback. Hidden/secret badges shown as ??? until unlocked (6 new secret badges). Library completion badges that auto-scale to any server (10/25/50/75/100% per library). Weekly/monthly/yearly recap page with top genres/directors/actors. Home screen widget showing rank + next 3 badges. Item detail page achievement ribbon. Daily login streak tracking with 4 loyalty badges. Director/actor affinity tracking with 6 people badges. Admin: custom user-defined badges via JSON, seasonal time-limited challenges, webhook notifications (Discord/Slack) on unlock, UI feature toggles. Search box and smart filters (unlocked/locked/close) on achievements page. Category leaderboards (score/movies/episodes/hours/streak/series). Shareable HTML profile card endpoint.

Add Eid Mubarak badge - unlocks on watches during Eid al-Fitr or Eid al-Adha. Dates hardcoded for 2024-2035 with a 5-day window around each anchor to cover the multi-day celebration and regional moon-sighting variance.

Massive badge expansion: 40+ new achievements across Film Festival, Season Devourer, Millennium, 3 AM Club, Momentum/Fortnight/Marathon Man (best-streak tiers), Time Traveller (decades), Globetrotter/World Tour (countries), Polyglot (languages), Genre Hopper, Epic Runtime, Ten/Hundred/500/Thousand Hour clubs, Christmas Cheer, New Year's Marathon, Halloween Night, Completionist+ (50+ ep series), Seen It All (100+ ep series), and Rewatcher tiers. Added admin badge enable/disable panel in plugin settings - disabled badges are hidden from user pages, excluded from completion %, leaderboard, server stats, and auto-unequipped. Live playback and backfill now capture production year, countries, language, genres, and runtime from items.

Fix live playback tracker silently dropping every event on Jellyfin 10.11 (reflection couldn't find UserId/SessionInfo because the real properties are Session and Users). Tracker now uses strong-typed PlaybackProgressEventArgs. Fix Explorer badge library detection - both backfill and live tracker now use ILibraryManager.GetCollectionFolders() instead of walking parents looking for CollectionFolder by class name. Live playback also now contributes to Explorer / UniqueLibrariesVisited.

Fix watch history backfill counting items but leaving badges locked at 0. AchievementBadgeService now normalizes user IDs to canonical dashed-GUID format at every entry point, and Load() migrates existing profiles so dashed/non-dashed duplicates are merged. Fix live playback tracker never registering - PlaybackCompletionTracker is now an IHostedService so its PlaybackProgress/PlaybackStopped subscriptions actually run. Added diagnostic logging for silent extraction failures.

Added watch history backfill - scans existing Jellyfin watch history to retroactively award badges. Added header badge icons next to profile picture. Admin page has new Scan Watch History and Scan All Users buttons.

Added equipped badge showcase in sidebar under Achievements entry. Badges appear as small colored pills with icons showing rarity border colors. New StarTrack-inspired banner with corner brackets, serif text glitch effect, and red accents.

Fixed standalone achievements page route detection. The page now correctly intercepts both #/achievements and #!/achievements hash formats. Sidebar uses click handler instead of href to set hash directly. Improved banner.

Separated admin and user pages. Sidebar now links to a standalone user-facing achievements page at #!/achievements that all users can access. Admin page with simulate/reset stays at Plugin Settings. Rewrote standalone.js with proper auth. Red and black banner.
Added automatic sidebar injection middleware so Achievements appears in the navigation menu for all users on every page load. No manual configuration needed - works like StarTrack's script injection.

Major rewrite. Removed admin-only restriction so all users can access achievements. Rewrote all API calls to use native fetch with proper auth token headers instead of broken apiClient.fetch(). Fixed simulate playback showing 'undefined' error. Fixed sidebar injection to use correct Jellyfin nav classes. Removed dependency on standalone.js hash routing.

Major bug fix release. Removed duplicate achievement definitions causing ambiguity. Fixed false playback recording on every session start inflating counters. Fixed GUID format inconsistency creating duplicate user profiles. Fixed leaderboard showing raw IDs instead of usernames. Fixed duplicate sidebar entries. Fixed unauthenticated API calls in profile inject. Replaced Console.WriteLine with proper ILogger. Removed unused Newtonsoft.Json dependency.

Sidebar, routing and simulate fix release. Added EnableInMainMenu so Achievements appears natively in the Jellyfin sidebar. Added client-script API endpoint so sidebar.js and standalone.js are served and injected into the shell on first plugin page visit. Fixed simulate-playback sending GET instead of POST. Fixed standalone.js using unauthenticated bare fetch instead of ApiClient. Fixed navinject.js pointing to a non-existent page name.

Build fix and stability release. Removed dead WebInjectionHostedService that caused a compile failure due to a missing RegisterAll method reference. SafeStartupRunner now handles all delayed injection safely. Plugin compiles cleanly with zero warnings.
Stability recovery release. Reworked File Transformation integration to use safe delayed execution, preventing Jellyfin startup failures while preserving sidebar and UI injection functionality.
Theme-aware sidebar integration fix.
File Transformation payload fix release.
Service registration and packaging recovery release.
Compatibility refactor release.
Sidebar injection troubleshooting release.
Shell and build fix release.
Global injection fix release.
Stability and regression fix release.
Major progression update.
Navigation and user-loading improvements.
Standalone achievements route.
Integration release.
Achievements dashboard redesign.
Personalisation and showcase update.
Major achievement system expansion.
Major UI stability update.
Backend improvements and badge persistence fixes.
Added embedded achievements page with badge viewer.
Added badge reset endpoint, summary endpoint and improved persistence.
Added playback simulation endpoint and improved badge logic.
Improved API structure and plugin stability.
Added API test endpoint for plugin verification.
Initial plugin release.
